tldr.title: It's Not Just X. It's Y
tldr.author: Yogthos
bullets: Supervised learning requires defining the target variable Y carefully, as optimizing the wrong label or metric undermines model utility.
Target leakage occurs when training features X inadvertently contain information about Y, artificially inflating validation scores but failing in production.
Successful AI deployment requires aligning Y with actual business outcomes rather than just maximizing statistical accuracy on arbitrary datasets.

tldr.title: revo, the programming language
tldr.author: alurm
bullets: Revo is a reversible programming language designed to execute computations both forwards and backwards with zero information loss.
It targets quantum computing simulation and energy-efficient thermodynamic computing paradigms by avoiding traditional state-destruction overhead.
The language introduces specialized syntax for invertible operations, ensuring deterministic bidirectional execution of control flows.

tldr.title: Pwnd Blaster: Hacking your PC using your speaker without ever touching it
tldr.author: matthew2
bullets: Software can remap audio jack functions, silently converting output speakers into microphones to eavesdrop on surrounding physical environments.
Air-gapped PCs are vulnerable to ultrasonic command injection and data exfiltration using standard speakers and nearby mobile receivers.
Targeted acoustic frequencies can cause physical resonance in hard drives, triggering read/write errors and system crashes without physical access.

tldr.title: github and the crime against software
tldr.author: abnercoimbre
bullets: GitHub centralizes Git, fostering vendor lock-in through proprietary PRs, non-standard APIs, and walled-garden developer collaboration tools.
Proprietary platforms like GitHub Actions limit CI/CD portability, making migration to open alternatives like GitLab or Forgejo highly labor-intensive.
DevOps engineers should adopt standard, decentralized Git workflows and self-hosted forges to preserve infrastructure autonomy and avoid single-vendor dependency.

tldr.title: Morningstar values SpaceX at $780B, half its IPO target
tldr.author: berkeleyjunk
bullets: Morningstar values SpaceX at $780B, half its IPO target
